metadata.task: Lunar lander safe pinpoint landing with minimal fuel.

condition.expression: left_contact and right_contact and abs(vy) < 0.2 and abs(vx) < 0.2 and abs(angle) < 0.1
(abs(x) > 0.99) or ((left_contact or right_contact) and (abs(vy) > 0.8 or abs(angle) > 0.8))

expected_failure_modes: Agent hovers indefinitely near x=0 to collect dense centering reward without landing.
Agent may repeatedly crash softly to exploit reset mechanism if crash penalty insufficient.
Agent might oscillate orientation to avoid upright penalty while not landing.
Low fuel penalty weight could lead to excessive throttling.
High descent speed near ground without leg contact may not be penalized enough if soft_descent weight is too low.
risk_notes: Centering reward could cause over-staying near center; ensure total episode budget motivates timely landing via fuel penalty and success bonus.
Crash penalty defined only on leg contact and extreme velocity/angle; may miss body-first crashes where legs never touch; relies on environment episodic termination to limit exploit.
Success threshold for touchdown: vx<0.2, vy<0.2, angle<0.1 might be too strict/lenient; monitor first successful landings.
No explicit reward for hovering close to ground; descent guidance only activates when y<0.5, gap between ground and flag position may require tuning.
Agent may develop 'suicidal' descents that trigger crash penalty only once but learn to get high centering reward before that, requiring careful weight balance.

bootstrap_report.design_rationale: The reward uses a centered position term for lateral guidance, a conditional descent-speed penalty for soft landing preparation, an upright stability term for attitude control, and a fuel-cost penalty for efficiency. Sparse events provide a large success bonus for simultaneous two-leg soft upright touchdown and a large crash penalty for extreme conditions or leaving the viewport. The combination is intended to shape the full approach sequence.
assumptions: Coordinates are normalized: x in approximately [-1,1], landing pad at (0,0) with flags nearby.
The lander starts with random initial thrust; dense rewards apply from the first step.
Left and right leg contact booleans reliably indicate ground contact.
The environment terminates episode on crash or exiting viewport, making sparse event triggers feasible.
Conditional component y<0.5 selects the lower half of the viewport where descent guidance matters.
risk_notes: The agent may exploit centering term by hovering; fuel penalty must be large enough to counteract.
No penalty for high horizontal velocity until touchdown; a fast skid could still satisfy success if vx low at contact.
Crash threshold (vy>0.8, angle>0.8) may be high, missing moderate crashes; later tuning will tighten limits.
Centering reward only covers x, not y proximity to pad; agent could be high and still collect reward, potentially delaying descent.
All dense components are linear/absolute-value based, which may cause high-speed jittering if weights are too high.
